name: auto:eligible
description: /auto MAY pick this issue for autonomous work. Required to claim.

name: auto:claimed
description: A lease is held by an /auto run (paired with a lease comment + assignee). Do not pick up.

name: auto:hold
description: Human-gated. /auto must NOT pick this issue even if otherwise eligible (used for escalations).

name: auto:stop
description: Kill-switch on the pinned #auto-control issue; presence halts all /auto pickup until removed.

name: auto:seeded
description: Issue was filed automatically by the --seed/triage pass. Carries a hidden fingerprint marker.

name: status:triage
description: Newly filed (often by --seed). Awaiting prioritization/sizing; not yet auto-pickable.

name: status:ready
description: Fully specced (size + priority + acceptance criteria). Ready to implement.

name: status:in-progress
description: Implementation underway in an /auto iteration.

name: status:in-review
description: PR open against develop-auto, in bounded review rounds.

name: status:done
description: PR merged to develop-auto. Issue closed by Closes #N.

name: status:blocked
description: Failed or blocked; needs a human. /auto will not proceed.

name: priority:P0
description: Critical. Drop everything (build broken, security, data loss). Highest priority.

name: priority:P1
description: High. Should be done this cycle.

name: priority:P2
description: Normal. Default priority.

name: priority:P3
description: Low / nice-to-have. Picked up only when the queue is otherwise empty.

name: type:feature
description: New user-facing capability.

name: type:bug
description: Defect: behavior diverges from intent/spec.

name: type:chore
description: Maintenance: deps, config, housekeeping with no behavior change.

name: type:spike
description: Time-boxed investigation; output is findings, not shipped behavior.

name: type:refactor
description: Internal restructuring; no functional change.

name: type:docs
description: Documentation-only change.

name: size:S
description: Small: < ~1h, single file/concern. Size is informational.

name: size:M
description: Medium: a few files, one concern. Size is informational.

name: size:L
description: Large: cross-cutting, multiple modules. Size is informational.

name: size:XL
description: Extra-large: should usually be split. Size is informational, not an engine selector.
